{"step_by_step_thinking": "The query asks whether body diffusion-weighted MR imaging is helpful in detecting uterine endometrial cancer in nonenhanced MR imaging. From the MEDCORP documents, several key points are relevant: Document [4] states that diffusion-weighted imaging can improve diagnostic accuracy, particularly for differentiating benign and malignant lesions. Document [15] specifies that diffusion-weighted sequences are included in the recommended MRI protocol for evaluating uterine lesions, especially when an adnexal mass is visualized. Document [20] and [21] emphasize that diffusion-weighted imaging is useful in evaluating adenomyosis and other uterine conditions, with specific mention of its role in detecting abnormal tissue. Additionally, diffusion-weighted imaging is described as 'invaluable' in assessing mass lesions within and around the uterus (Document [0]). These findings indicate that diffusion-weighted imaging provides valuable information in identifying malignant lesions, including endometrial cancer, even in nonenhanced MRI sequences. Therefore, based on the evidence, diffusion-weighted imaging is indeed helpful in detecting cancer in nonenhanced MR imaging.", "answer_choice": "A"}
